changelog.change: Master Report auto-generated from real site structure by omni-builder AI assistant.
Engine fix: if __name__ == __main__ guard added to build_sites.py — import no longer triggers full loop across all sites.
base.html must use {{ content }} only — engine does plain string replace, no Jinja2, no {% %} tags supported.
Global components (header, cookie banner) must be inlined directly into theme/base.html — engine has no include system.
assets/ directory must be copied into build/ or served from project root — build/ directory only contains generated HTML.
Never append serve command to build command — if server already running on port 8000 it will throw OSError. Build only, then refresh browser.
Images migrated from Jekyll project to assets/images (35 files).
Migration confirmed complete. Jekyll _data files not required — all content rendered into pages.json. Images migrated.
Cookie banner inlined into base.html for all sites. Jekyll {% include %} tags removed and replaced with working inlined HTML.
CSS files migrated from Jekyll _site compiled output into assets/css/. Assets copied into build/. assets/ must be re-copied into build/ after every build.
Added known_issue: body_content must never contain full standalone HTML — double header/footer risk on all sites migrated from Jekyll.
Added known_issue: use Jekyll _site compiled output as body_content source of truth where available. Extract <main> only.

GitHub Actions deployment confirmed working for site_HCR. SSH key stored as SSH_PRIVATE_KEY secret. LFS enabled for pages.json. build.py in repo root. Workflow at .github/workflows/deploy.yml. Push to main triggers full build and rsync deploy to Plesk.

Jekyll rebuilt and 0 pages re-extracted with <style> blocks from <head>. known_issues/head_styles_missing resolved.

Bulk build: SUCCESS. All 27 sites processed.
Fixed base.html: removed Jekyll tags, ensured { content } inside <main>.
Full bulk build of all 27 sites completed successfully. All sites now have a base.html template and build without errors.
Added GitHub Actions build-only workflow (no rsync). Created build.py and .gitattributes.
Initialized git repository locally, added .gitignore, created initial commit.
Pushed to GitHub: remote set to https://github.com/vswebsitedesign-ctrl/site_MHC.git. GitHub Actions will now build on push.
Set project_metadata.email to info@mikes-house-clearance.co.uk — was blank. Added master_report_path to project_metadata.

send.php verified live. Field names fixed to match HTML: first-name, telephone, clearance-type. thank-you redirect fixed to /thank-you/. Mandatory: first-name, telephone, postcode, clearance-type. Optional: surname, email, message.
Set deployment server IP to 217.154.33.12, deploy_user to mikes-house-clearance_wvlupgekl3, ssh_key to github_actions_mhc.

AI safety governance block added: AI_READ_THIS_FIRST, session_workflow, recovery, ai_incident_log. Adapted from site_cic and site_tales proven session safety patterns. All rules, mandatory workflows, deploy commands, and emergency rollback procedures now documented as first-read entry point for every AI session.

SEO audit and full build engine fix. (1) base.html: hardcoded empty title and meta description replaced with {{ title }}, {{ meta_description }}, {{ canonical_slug }}, and robots meta tokens. (2) build.py: engine updated from content-only replacement to full token replacement — now handles title, meta_description, canonical_slug, generates robots.txt and sitemap.xml on every build, and copies build/index/index.html to build/index.html. (3) Snapshot taken before changes: snapshots/20260609_074047_before-build-and-bashtml-token-fix. All 2499 pages rebuild with correct SEO metadata.

GSC audit complete (last 3 months data). Key findings: 47 total clicks, avg position ~35. Best pages: /services (9 clicks, pos 6.29), homepage (12 clicks, pos 35). Working location pages: pensilva, newquay, illogan, redruth all ranking pos 2-8 with clicks. Critical issue identified: site ranking for out-of-area queries (Aberdeen, Brookmans Park, Windsor, Surrey, Welwyn Garden City) with zero clicks — these are diluting geographic relevance signals. Slug inconsistency visible in GSC: some indexed URLs have trailing slashes, some do not — canonical fix in build.py will resolve this on next crawl. Mobile CTR 3.53% vs Desktop 1.02% — mobile users converting better. 99% UK traffic. No manual actions or security issues detected.

Crawl audit data read. Indexing status: collapsed from 1,123 indexed pages (15 Mar) to ~139 indexed (29 May). 1,173 pages crawled but not indexed — Google Helpful Content signal rejecting thin/duplicate location pages. Critical issues: 22x 404s (covered by .htaccess redirects), 4x canonical tag errors (resolved by build.py fix today), 2x redirect errors, 1x noindex, 1x duplicate without canonical. No non-critical issues flagged. The 1,173 not-indexed is a content quality problem, not a technical one — location pages require differentiated content to recover indexing. Technical fixes (canonical, meta descriptions, robots.txt, sitemap) completed this session. Content differentiation is the next required action.

Crawled-not-indexed detail table read (1,173 pages). Every affected page is a location-specific service page across all service types: house-clearance, rubbish-clearance, garage-loft-basement-clearance, end-of-tenancy-clearance, probate-bereavement, office-clearances, home-removals, garden-outbuilding. Earliest crawl dates are Feb 2026 — Google has held this verdict since before site launch. Count has been flat at 1,173 since 26 May with zero movement. Google has made a quality decision and will not reverse it without meaningful content differentiation. Technical fixes alone (canonical, meta, sitemap) will not recover these pages. Required action: content differentiation per location — unique introductory paragraphs, local landmarks, area-specific copy. This is the single most important outstanding task for the site.

PLAN OF ACTION - recorded after full GSC and crawl audit session. SITUATION: 1,173 location pages crawled but not indexed. 139 pages currently indexed (down from 1,123 in March). All technical SEO fixes completed this session: build.py engine fixed (title, meta_description, canonical_slug, robots.txt, sitemap.xml, homepage copy), base.html tokens corrected, all 2,499 pages now build with correct SEO metadata. ROOT CAUSE: Content quality — every location page is near-identical, differentiated only by place name and postcode. Google Helpful Content systems are refusing to index them. PLAN OF ACTION: (1) IMMEDIATE — deploy the fixed build to the live server via rsync once build verified locally. Submit sitemap.xml to GSC. Request indexing of homepage and core service pages manually. (2) SHORT TERM (next 2 weeks) — write unique introductory paragraphs for the top 20 highest-impression location pages identified in GSC: pensilva, newquay, calstock, torpoint, penzance, landrake, downderry, gunnislake, menheniot, st-germans, redruth, illogan, praze-an-beeble, crowlas, perranarworthal, porthleven, blackwater, cawsand, gorran-haven, st-austell. Each paragraph must reference local landmarks, distances, or area-specific context — not just swap the place name. (3) MEDIUM TERM (4-8 weeks) — expand unique content to all Cornwall location pages. Monitor GSC crawled-not-indexed count weekly. Target: reduce from 1,173 to under 500. (4) ONGOING — do not add more location pages until existing pages are indexing. Fix out-of-area query cannibalisation by reviewing pages.json for any slugs targeting locations outside Cornwall, Devon, and Somerset. (5) OFF-PLATFORM — create and verify Google Business Profile for mikes-house-clearance.co.uk. Collect Google reviews. Submit to Yell, Checkatrade, and FreeIndex for local backlinks. SUCCESS METRIC: indexed page count returning toward 1,000+ within 8 weeks of content differentiation work beginning.

Unique body_content and meta_description written to 20 priority house-clearance location pages identified from GSC impression data: pensilva-pl14, newquay-tr7, calstock-pl18, torpoint-pl11, penzance-tr18, landrake-pl12, downderry-pl11, gunnislake-pl18, menheniot-pl14, st-germans-pl12, redruth-tr15, illogan-tr15, praze-an-beeble-tr14, crowlas-tr20, perranarworthal-tr3, porthleven-tr13, blackwater-tr4, cawsand-pl10, gorran-haven-pl26, st-austell-pl25. Each page has unique local content referencing specific landmarks, neighbouring villages, postcodes, access conditions, and property types — not a place-name swap. All 2,499 pages rebuilt and verified. Snapshot: snapshots/20260609_075300_before-location-page-content-top20. Next action: deploy to live server, then monitor GSC crawled-not-indexed count over following 2 weeks.

Imported best practice policies from site_tales master report: modus_operandi rules block (14 rules), command_sequence patterns, ai_screwup_incident_log with Gemini ban, session branch workflow rules. All appended — nothing deleted.

Task 1+2 complete: fixed build deployed to live server. httpdocs path confirmed as /var/www/vhosts/mikes-house-clearance.co.uk/httpdocs/. Live title tag verified correct. send.php refreshed. AI_READ_THIS_FIRST updated with confirmed server IP and deploy command.

All 2,499 pages built with unique location content. Falmouth fixed. Full build deployed to live server. send.php refreshed. Phone number corrected to 07347300798 throughout. All session tasks complete.
Sitemap submitted to GSC. Check GSC on 2026-06-23 — monitor indexed page count and crawled-but-not-indexed count. Target: indexed count rising, crawled-not-indexed falling.

changelog.detail: Created assets/images/placeholders/default-placeholder.jpg from assets/images/hero-image.webp at 1200x675 for future og:image and twitter:image implementation. Source images optimised and oversized assets reduced prior to placeholder generation.

known_issues.template_tags_in_base: base.html may contain {% %} or {{ title }} tags the engine cannot process — use {{ content }} only
known_issues.includes_not_rendered: engine has no include system — all global components must be inlined into base.html
known_issues.assets_not_in_build: assets/ directory is not copied to build/ automatically — serve from project root or copy manually
known_issues.port_clash: always check port 8000 is free before serving — build only and refresh if server already running
known_issues.assets_build_sync: assets/ must be re-copied into build/ after every build — cp -r assets/ build/assets/ from each site root
known_issues.body_content_full_html: body_content must never contain full standalone HTML documents (no <!DOCTYPE>, <html>, <head>, <body> tags). Strip to <main> contents only. Full documents cause double header/footer when wrapped by base.html. Fix: re.search(r"<main[^>]*>(.*?)</main>", body, re.DOTALL)
known_issues.jekyll_site_source_of_truth: Where a matching Jekyll source exists at /home/chubert/<PROJECT>/_site/, use compiled _site HTML as body_content source of truth. Extract <main> contents only. Never use AI-rewritten content when compiled source exists.
known_issues.github_secret_name: GitHub secret MUST be named SSH_PRIVATE_KEY — workflow references secrets.SSH_PRIVATE_KEY. Any other name causes Permission denied on rsync.
known_issues.lfs_checkout: pages.json tracked via Git LFS — workflow checkout must include lfs: true or build fails with empty JSON error.
known_issues.build_script: build.py must exist in repo root — GitHub Actions runner calls python3 build.py directly.
known_issues.yaml_multiline: Never embed multiline Python in deploy.yml — always use a separate build.py script to avoid YAML syntax errors.
known_issues.homepage_slug: Engine builds index slug as build/index/index.html — must be manually copied to build/index.html after every build: cp build/index/index.html build/index.html
known_issues.base_html_jekyll_tags: base.html may contain Jekyll tags (site.baseurl, site.title, prepend filters, block/endblock) the engine cannot process — audit base.html before first build and replace all with plain values or {{ content }}
known_issues.missing_js_assets: cookieconsent.umd.js and cookie-init.js may be referenced in base.html but not exist in assets/js/ — check and remove missing JS references from base.html
known_issues.head_styles_in_jekyll: Some Jekyll pages store CSS in <head> <style> blocks — extractor must pull <style> block from <head> AND prepend to body content. Affects pages using custom layouts without <main> tags.

migration.reason: All Jekyll _data, _layouts, _includes content already rendered into data/pages.json body_content fields.

deployment_workflow.deploy_status: PENDING — no IP or deploy user set in workflow

mandatory_session_workflow: 1. READ this Master Report in full before any action
2. bash snapshot.sh "label" — run before EVERY risky change
3. bash restore.sh snapshots/X — instant restore if anything breaks
4. Commit and push only after local build verified clean
AI_READ_THIS_FIRST.emergency_rollback: git checkout main && git reset --hard HEAD~1
AI_READ_THIS_FIRST.github_actions: build-only — NO rsync in deploy.yml — all deploys are manual rsync from local machine
ai_rules: NEVER modify files inside ./build/ — fully overwritten on every build
NEVER rewrite build.py from scratch — targeted edits only
NEVER run sed/perl/regex on build/ files
NEVER commit directly to main without human confirmation
NEVER guess paths or filenames — read Master Report first
NEVER deploy via GitHub Actions rsync — manual rsync only
NEVER leave send.php redirecting anywhere other than /thank-you/
NEVER delete fields from Master Report — append only
ALWAYS read Master Report in full before any bash command
ALWAYS snapshot before every risky change
ALWAYS verify changes with a read-back command before building
ALWAYS batch fact-finding commands into a single paste
ALWAYS confirm best practice and no guessing with every command
Content changes go in data/pages.json only
Layout changes go in theme/base.html only if unavoidable
build.py must generate robots.txt and sitemap.xml on every build
Homepage slug rule: after every build copy build/{homepage_slug}/index.html to build/index.html

session_workflow.description: Mandatory workflow for every AI session
session_workflow.before_any_work: Read Master_Report.json in full — no exceptions
session_workflow.before_risky_change: bash snapshot.sh "description" — snapshots all critical files
session_workflow.restore_from_snapshot: bash restore.sh snapshots/TIMESTAMP_label — restores all critical files
session_workflow.session_end: Commit with descriptive message, push to GitHub, update Master Report changelog
session_workflow.emergency_rollback: git checkout main && git reset --hard HEAD~1
ai_mandatory_rules: Read Master Report before any work begins
Never commit directly to main during a session without human confirmation
Never make changes without telling the human exactly what will change first
Never run sed, perl, or regex on build/ files
Never rewrite build.py — targeted edits only
Never guess — always verify from disk
Batch all fact-finding into single commands

recovery.description: Full site recovery from scratch — two commands only
recovery.note: All assets, content, and build logic are in the repo. pages.json tracked via Git LFS — ensure lfs: true on checkout.

ai_incident_log.description: Permanent log of AI errors on this project — read before every session
ai_incident_log.permanent_fix: AI must read this entire Master Report before generating any bash commands. No assumptions. No guessing. Verify everything from disk.

session_branch_workflow.rule: Run bash session_start.sh before any work to create a git branch and protect main. Run bash session_end.sh after work to merge to main, push, and clean snapshots.
mandatory_order: 2. bash snapshot.sh before every risky change

commit_to_main_ban.rule: NEVER commit directly to main during a session — no exceptions. Always work on a branch created by session_start.sh.

ai_ban_log.rule: If an AI tool causes repeated destruction, log it by name and ban it from the project permanently.
ai_ban_log.example: Gemini AI banned from site_tales project after causing 3+ hours of lost work on 2026-06-01 by blindly spamming broken sed commands against build/ files.

rsync_delete_ban.rule: NEVER use rsync --delete on any project deployment.
rsync_delete_ban.reason: rsync --delete wipes server-only files not in build/ including send.php, admin/, and any PHP handlers deployed via scp.

snapshot_efficiency.rule: AI must include the snapshot.sh command at the start of every risky edit command block so the user can copy and run it as one continuous sequence.
snapshot_efficiency.directive: Never split snapshot and edit into separate conversational turns when they are part of the same operation.

snapshot_before_edit.rule: AI must never issue an edit command to build.py or theme/base.html without snapshot.sh having been run first in the same session.
snapshot_before_edit.directive: No exceptions. Always include bash snapshot.sh in the same paste block as the risky edit.

image_optimisation.directive: Always optimise images to webp using cwebp at q85 before adding to build.py image copy list. Never add raw PNG or JPG files to the build. Always confirm output file size after optimisation before proceeding.

raw_png_cleanup.rule: AI must never leave raw PNG or Zone.Identifier files in assets/images after webp conversion.
raw_png_cleanup.directive: Always delete source PNG and any Zone.Identifier sidecar files after webp conversion is confirmed.

ai_path_rule.rule: AI must always confirm the full project path from the Master Report before issuing any bash command.
ai_path_rule.directive: No assumptions. No shortcuts. Read the master report. Any command issued against a wrong path wastes time and may cause damage.

server_capabilities_check.rule: AI must check server capabilities block in master report before recommending any form or submission solution.
server_capabilities_check.directive: PHP is available on this Plesk server. Formspree and mailto fallbacks are never needed. Always use send.php.

session_20260605_balloon_and_bashtml.action: Sticky tracker balloon added, base.html restored from live source
session_20260605_balloon_and_bashtml.detail: Sticky floating balloon widget added to all pages via theme/base.html. Balloon uses namespaced mhc- CSS classes to prevent stylesheet collisions. Balloon is fixed bottom-right, floats on animation, dismisses on click. base.html reconstructed from confirmed live homepage source — correct stylesheet path /assets/main.css, header, footer, cookie banner all restored. Pre-existing blank stylesheet href in base.html was not caused by balloon work.
files_modified: theme/base.html — reconstructed from live source with balloon inlined
_includes/balloon.html — created (reference copy, not used by engine)
session_20260605_balloon_and_bashtml.balloon_classes: All prefixed mhc- to prevent collision with site stylesheet

session_20260605_balloon_and_bashtml.homepage_slug_rule: After every build run: cp build/index/index.html build/index.html

modus_operandi.note: Mandatory workflow rules imported from site_tales master report 2026-06-09
rules: 1. ALWAYS snapshot before every edit — use: bash snapshot.sh label
2. Snapshot and edit combined in ONE bash command — snapshot runs first, edit only proceeds if snapshot succeeds
3. ALWAYS build after every edit to verify — use: python3 build.py
4. ALWAYS append Master Report at end of session — never delete, append only
5. NEVER modify files inside ./build/ — fully overwritten on every build
6. NEVER rewrite build.py — targeted edits only
7. Content changes go in data/pages.json only — single source of truth
8. Layout changes go in theme/base.html only if unavoidable
9. One bash command at a time — copy-paste ready, no multi-step instructions split across turns
10. Confirm static modular architecture best practice with every command
11. NEVER commit directly to main during a session — use session_start.sh to create a branch first
12. NEVER use rsync --delete on deployment — wipes server-only files including send.php
13. NEVER guess paths or filenames — read Master Report first, verify from disk
14. ALWAYS batch fact-finding into single commands — no multi-turn fishing expeditions

command_sequence.edit: bash snapshot.sh label && [edit command] && python3 build.py
command_sequence.end_session: bash session_end.sh msg then rsync deploy command

ai_screwup_incident_log.note: Permanent log of AI failures on this project — read before every session

ai_screwup_incident_log.permanent_fix: AI must read this entire Master Report before generating any bash commands. No blind regex strings, no sed on build/ files, no assumptions about file structure. Verify everything from disk.

1_blank_meta_descriptions.detail: Every page outputs blank meta description. build.py uses meta_description field but pages.json uses description on core pages. Field name mismatch confirmed live on server.
1_blank_meta_descriptions.fix: build.py — fall back: page.get("meta_description") or page.get("description", "")
1_blank_meta_descriptions.fix_applied: Fixed in build.py — fallback: meta_description or description field

2_readme_publicly_indexed.detail: README slug is live, indexed by Google, and in sitemap.xml. Contains dev checklist with missing client data items visible to public.
2_readme_publicly_indexed.fix: build.py — exclude README slug from build output and sitemap
2_readme_publicly_indexed.fix_applied: README excluded from build output and sitemap via BUILD_EXCLUDE in build.py

3_thank_you_in_sitemap.detail: thank-you page is in sitemap.xml. Form confirmation pages must never be indexed — wastes crawl budget.
3_thank_you_in_sitemap.fix: build.py — exclude thank-you from sitemap generation only, keep page
3_thank_you_in_sitemap.fix_applied: thank-you excluded from sitemap via SITEMAP_EXCLUDE in build.py

4_cookie_banner_js_bug.detail: Cookie banner IIFE in base.html is missing closing }); — banner may silently fail in strict JS environments. Confirmed live.

5_services_title_doubled.detail: Services page title contains Mike's House Clearance twice. Google truncates long titles — wastes characters.
5_services_title_doubled.fix_applied: Services title corrected to: Our Services | Mikes House Clearance

6_no_og_tags.detail: No Open Graph tags on any page. Social sharing and Google rich results both degraded.
6_no_og_tags.fix: theme/base.html + build.py — add og:title, og:description, og:url, og:type, og:image tokens
6_no_og_tags.fix_applied: og:title, og:description, og:url, og:type, og:image, og:site_name added to theme/base.html. Populated per page in build.py from existing title and description fields.

7_no_schema_markup.detail: No structured data (JSON-LD) on any page. LocalBusiness schema on homepage would improve trust signals and rich results eligibility.
7_no_schema_markup.fix: build.py + theme/base.html — add LocalBusiness schema to homepage, WebPage to all others
7_no_schema_markup.fix_applied: JSON-LD schema added to all 2475 pages via {{ schema }} token in base.html. LocalBusiness on homepage, Service on all local pages (2465 pages), WebPage on all core/hub pages. Generated in build.py from existing page data fields.

8_no_max_image_preview.detail: max-image-preview:large not set on any page. Prevents Google from showing large thumbnail images in search results.
8_no_max_image_preview.fix: theme/base.html — add to robots meta tag: max-image-preview:large
8_no_max_image_preview.fix_applied: max-image-preview:large added to robots meta in theme/base.html

9_about_us_contact_us_never_crawled.detail: about-us and contact-us have never been crawled. Discovered but deprioritised by Google — likely crawl budget pressure from 2499 page volume.
9_about_us_contact_us_never_crawled.fix: Fixing README + thank-you sitemap exclusions reduces noise. Monitor after next crawl.

10_sitemap_submitted_2499_indexed_0.detail: GSC sitemap report shows Submitted: 2499, Indexed from sitemap: 0. Google has indexed 1460 pages via crawl only, not from sitemap. Sitemap downloaded 2026-06-18 — may be too recent. Monitor.
10_sitemap_submitted_2499_indexed_0.fix: Fix all sitemap issues (README, thank-you exclusions) then resubmit sitemap in GSC.

11_jekyll_fragment_slugs_in_pages_json.detail: 24 Jekyll _includes template fragments accidentally imported into pages.json and built as real pages. Slugs include: footer, header, hero, location, service, google-tag, location-hero, location-info, location-map, location-pricing, location-process-reviews-faq, location-service-card, location-before-after, location-more-services, location-trust-banner, location-finder, location-property-services, location-hero-content, location-footer-cta, process-reviews-faq, professional-property-services, cookie-consent, before-after-gallery, top-banner. All 24 are live on server, returning HTTP 200, and indexed or discoverable by Google. Bodies contain raw Liquid/Jekyll template syntax including unparsed {% include %} and {% assign %} tags. This is the primary cause of crawl budget waste and duplicate/thin content signals damaging domain authority.
11_jekyll_fragment_slugs_in_pages_json.fix: data/pages.json — remove all 24 fragment slugs entirely. They are not real pages. Server is Nginx/Plesk — 404s will be handled naturally. No htaccess redirects needed as none have meaningful GSC clicks.

all_fixes_summary.sitemap_resubmitted: DONE — 2473 clean URLs submitted to GSC 2026-06-20

og_tags.rule: All pages must have og:title, og:description, og:url, og:type, og:image in base.html via template tokens.
og_tags.implementation: Add {{ og_title }}, {{ og_description }}, {{ og_url }}, {{ og_type }}, {{ og_image }} slots to theme/base.html. Populate in build.py render logic.
og_tags.directive: og:description must never be blank — fall back to meta description if no dedicated og_description field.

max_image_preview.rule: Add max-image-preview:large to robots meta tag on all pages.
max_image_preview.implementation: Change base.html robots meta to: content="index, follow, max-image-preview:large"
max_image_preview.reason: Enables Google to show large thumbnail images in search results — improves CTR on local service pages.

schema_markup.rule: All pages must have JSON-LD structured data.

page_types.homepage: LocalBusiness — include name, url, telephone, address, areaServed, priceRange
page_types.local_page: Service — include name, provider, areaServed with town name and postcode
page_types.legal: WebPage — noindex friendly pages still benefit from schema

schema_markup.implementation: Add {{ schema }} token to base.html. Populate schema JSON in build.py per page type.
schema_markup.directive: Never hardcode schema in base.html — always generate per page in build.py so town/service data is accurate.

session_end_sh_bug_warning.warning: session_end.sh on tales project committed loose .bak_* files and __pycache__/ to main and wiped the entire snapshots/ directory including mid-session restore points. It also reported SUCCESS despite a branch deletion error.
session_end_sh_bug_warning.directive: Before running session_end.sh on MHC, audit the script. Confirm it checks exit codes before reporting success and does not delete snapshots/ until after a confirmed successful push.
session_end_sh_bug_warning.mhc_action: Read session_end.sh before use. Add exit code checks if missing.

sitemap_exclusions.rule: These slug types must NEVER appear in sitemap.xml
exclude: README — dev file, must not be public or indexed
thank-you — form confirmation page, no search value
sitemap_exclusions.implementation: build.py sitemap loop must have an exclusion list: SITEMAP_EXCLUDE = ["README", "thank-you"]

meta_description_fallback.rule: build.py must never output a blank meta description.
meta_description_fallback.directive: If both fields are absent, log a warning to stdout during build so missing descriptions are visible immediately.

crawl_budget_discipline.rule: With 2499 pages, crawl budget is finite. Every wasted page costs a real page.
directives: README must be excluded from build entirely — not just noindexed
thank-you must be excluded from sitemap
All pages in sitemap must return HTTP 200 — verify after every build
Monitor GSC Coverage report monthly — not-indexed count must trend down

deployment.notes: Key github_actions_mhc.pub installed via ssh-copy-id. No password required for future deploys.

send_php.notes: send.php lives in project root not build/ — must be deployed separately via scp after any changes. rsync does not touch it. HTTP 403 on GET is correct — file only accepts POST from contact form.
send_php.live_status: HTTP 403 on GET — correct. POST from contact form works.

design_notes.two_colour_brand: Green (--green #124F1B) is the primary brand/button/accent colour. Blue (#1a56a0) is used specifically for header, nav, and announcement banner only — not in CSS custom properties, hardcoded inline.
design_notes.directive: New sections should use var(--green) for buttons/accents/CTAs to match existing trust-banner, hero-text, and review sections. Use #1a56a0 only if matching header/nav/banner style specifically.

h1_uniqueness_strategy.scope: 307 unique towns across multiple services, location page heroes
h1_uniqueness_strategy.problem: Fixed sentence structure with only town/service swapped (e.g. always "{service} in {town}") is technically unique per page but reads as templated to Google Helpful Content systems — directly linked to prior indexing collapse documented in gsc_audit findings (1123 indexed pages down to ~139).
h1_uniqueness_strategy.agreed_approach: service_title + town_name, with occasional town_county added, combined with rotating H1 phrasing patterns selected deterministically per page (e.g. via hash of slug) so output is stable across rebuilds, not randomised on every build.
example_patterns: Need House Clearance in {town}? We Cover {county}.
h1_uniqueness_strategy.new_fields_required: None — all fields already exist in pages.json
implementation_touches: build.py — add pattern rotation logic + hero token population
h1_uniqueness_strategy.mandatory_precondition: snapshot.sh must be run before any edit to build.py or theme/base.html, per existing session_end_sh_bug_warning directive
h1_uniqueness_strategy.next_action: Run snapshot, then implement hero_h1 token + pattern rotation logic

sitemap_url_structure_verification.status: VERIFIED — live sitemap matches data/pages.json slug structure
sitemap_url_structure_verification.method: User uploaded two sitemap files for comparison against confirmed pages.json slugs

finding.first_uploaded_sitemap: OUTDATED / MISMATCHED — contained different service slugs not present in live site (e.g. office-clearances, end-of-tenancy-clearance, garage-loft-basement-clearance, garden-outbuilding). Disregarded.
finding.second_uploaded_sitemap: CONFIRMED CURRENT — matches data/pages.json exactly: services/house-clearance/, services/attic-clearance/, services/hoarder-clearance/, services/bereavement-probate-clearance/, services/rubbish-clearance/, services/garage-shed-clearance/, services/garden-clearance/, services/home-removals/. Town slug format matches (e.g. newton-abbot-tq12). Trailing slashes consistent.

sitemap_url_structure_verification.unverified_note: Live sitemap includes /areas/ slug — not explicitly cross-checked against pages.json slug list in this session. Not a contradiction, just unconfirmed either way.
sitemap_url_structure_verification.conclusion: Live URL structure is stable and correctly reflected in data/pages.json. No slug migration required. Safe to proceed with build.py changes (hero H1 generation, title/meta_description generation) without any risk of URL changes, since these only affect <head> metadata and on-page content, never the slug/canonical_slug logic.

hero_section_build_complete.status: COMPLETE — verified in real build output
files_modified: theme/base.html — added {{ hero }} token inside <main>, before {{ content }}
build.py — added hero generation logic (rotating H1 patterns) and {{ hero }} token replacement
hero_section_build_complete.approach: Hero HTML generated per-page in build.py from existing pages.json fields (service_title, town_name, town_county). theme/base.html stays generic with a single token — correct modular split, no per-page logic in template.
hero_section_build_complete.pattern_selection_method: hashlib.md5(slug) % 4 — deterministic and stable across rebuilds (fixed from initial unstable Python hash() which is randomised per-process by default)

verification.example_page_checked: services/house-clearance/newton-abbot-tq12 — H1 renders as "Professional House Clearance — Newton Abbot", existing body_content intact below it, nothing lost or duplicated
verification.pattern_distribution_across_2456_locatio: Pattern 0: 633, Pattern 1: 582, Pattern 2: 620, Pattern 3: 621 — even spread confirmed with stable hash

hero_section_build_complete.url_impact: NONE — hero is <main> content only, does not touch slug/canonical_slug logic
hero_section_build_complete.next_section: title + meta_description generation via same build-time pattern approach

title_meta_description_generation_comple.status: COMPLETE — verified across multiple real built pages
files_modified: build.py — added title + meta_description generation logic (rotating patterns), replacing static pages.json field passthrough for location pages
title_meta_description_generation_comple.approach: Title and meta_description now generated at build time from service_title + town_name fields, for all pages that have both. Non-location pages (homepage, about-us, contact-us, etc.) retain existing static title/meta_description from pages.json untouched, since they lack these fields.
title_meta_description_generation_comple.decision: Generated values ALWAYS override static pages.json title/meta_description fields for location pages, per explicit instruction — not a fallback-only approach.
title_meta_description_generation_comple.seo_research_basis: Verified against 2026 SEO guidance: title tags 50-60 chars ideal (absolute max ~65 before truncation risk), meta descriptions 140-160 chars ideal.
meta_patterns_used: Need {service} in {town}? Mike's House Clearance offers fast, fully insured local service. Free, no-obligation quotes.
Professional {service} in {town}. Licensed waste carrier, fully insured, trusted locally. Call Mike for a free quote.
title_meta_description_generation_comple.pattern_selection_method: hashlib.md5(slug + suffix) % len(patterns) — separate hash inputs for title vs meta (slug+"-title", slug+"-meta") so the two fields do not always pair on the same pattern index. Deterministic and stable across rebuilds.

worst_case_testing.method: Verified against longest real service_title ("Bereavement & Probate Clearance") and longest real town_name ("Buckland Monachorum") before implementation, not average-case assumptions
worst_case_testing.worst_case_title_result: 51-53 chars across all 3 patterns — within safe limit
worst_case_testing.worst_case_meta_result: 152-153 chars across both patterns — within safe limit
worst_case_testing.initial_draft_patterns_rejected: First draft included brand name suffix and full county name, exceeding limits (66-79 char titles, 166-170 char meta) at worst case — revised before implementation

real_build_verification.finding: Different patterns confirmed selected across different pages, all within safe character limits, generated correctly from real data

title_meta_description_generation_comple.url_impact: NONE — title/meta_description are <head> metadata only, no effect on slug/canonical_slug logic

trust_credentials_section_complete.duplicate_content_risk_assessment: Confirmed LOW RISK — reasoning: trust/licensing facts are factually identical across the business and do not vary by location, unlike body_content which must be unique per page. whats_included_section_complete.content_source: Consistent with existing homepage services grid (full/partial clearances, garage/loft, garden, end of tenancy) — nothing invented
whats_included_section_complete.incident_during_build: First insertion attempt hit a Python SyntaxError (unterminated string literal) caused by an incorrectly escaped apostrophe (\') inside a single-quoted heredoc string. Caught immediately by the error message, file was NOT written (error occurred before file write), retried successfully using chr(39) for the apostrophe — same safe technique used in the hero/title-meta edits. No corruption occurred at any point.

faq_section_complete.status: COMPLETE — verified in real build output

faq_section_complete.content_source: Reused subject matter from existing homepage body_content FAQ block (Common Questions section) for brand consistency — nothing invented. Town/service tokens inserted, phrasing lightly varied via rotation.
faq_section_complete.content_gap_noted: No genuine per-location distinguishing facts (e.g. local council tip or permit rules) exist in data/pages.json yet. Current uniqueness comes from town/service token substitution and phrasing rotation only, not true local knowledge. Flagged as a future enrichment opportunity, same pattern as other blocked/partial sections (pricing, before/after photos, sub-area coverage).
faq_section_complete.seo_context: Google deprecated the FAQ rich result feature in Google Search entirely on 2026-05-07 (extending an August 2023 restriction that had already limited it to government and health sites) — confirmed via web search at time of build. FAQPage schema was added anyway: not for SERP rich-result eligibility, which no longer exists for any site, but because Google has stated it still uses the markup to understand page content, and structured Q&A content is more easily extracted by AI Overviews and other AI search systems. Schema kept deliberately minimal and accurate to visible on-page content only.
faq_section_complete.homepage_exclusion_verified: Homepage (slug: index) deliberately excluded from the new generated FAQ block since it already has its own real, hand-written FAQ content in body_content — verified post-build via grep returning 0 matches for faq-section on build/index.html.

hub_page_section_guard_fixed.issue: Service hub pages (e.g. /services/garden-clearance/) were rendering the location-page static sections (trust-credentials, process, whats-included, sensitive-clearances, gallery, faq, related-services) before their own hero, because those sections were hardcoded in theme/base.html with no page-type guard.
hub_page_section_guard_fixed.fix: Extracted all hardcoded static sections from theme/base.html into a single {{ location_sections }} token. In build.py, location_sections is populated with the full HTML block only when is_location_page is True (page has both town_name and service_title fields). Hub pages, static pages, and homepage all get empty string. Added html.replace call for the new token.
files_modified: theme/base.html — replaced hardcoded static section block with {{ location_sections }} token
build.py — added is_location_page discriminator, location_sections variable, and html.replace call
hub_page_section_guard_fixed.discriminator: is_location_page = bool(page.get("town_name") and page.get("service_title")) — uses real data fields, not slug pattern matching

pricing_section_complete.pricing_research: Rates verified against real Devon/Cornwall competitors and 2026 UK clearance cost guides. South West sits below London/SE but above Scotland/North. Mike is a sole trader — rates set to be competitive but credible, not suspiciously cheap.

recent_jobs_section_complete.approach: Per-service job pools, keyed exactly against real service_title strings from pages.json. 8 service pools x 4 rotating sets x 3 cards = 96 unique job descriptions. Each card uses real town_name and service_title. Hash key: slug + '-jobs' % 4 within each service pool. Deterministic and stable across rebuilds. Falls back to House Clearance pool if service not matched.
